title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 18 - The Enemies! - Part 1
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GgtIBQCZxkQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) launches into one of Shadows of Brimstone's greatest strengths: its enormous enemy variety. He praises how all essential enemy information fits on a single card—no flipping through bestiaries mid-combat—making it easy to build a threat deck with twenty different enemies and still play smoothly.

Four deluxe enemy packs get the spotlight. The Serpentmen of Jargano bring tribal snake warriors with a grand shaman casting juju spells, voodoo trinkets, and multiple serpent tribes. The Masters of the Void introduce void hounds whose defence scales with initiative (punishing fast heroes and rewarding slow ones) and void sorcerers casting variable-level spells. The Undead Outlaws deliver Daniel's favourite miniature in the entire game—the Undead Gunslinger, a legendary duellist who challenges heroes to face-offs with the fastest dead in the west. Finally, the Omaraki Carrion Phoenix breathes fire via template attacks, spawns plague-ridden Scourge Dead that rise from corpses, and spreads vile diseases. Daniel notes how later expansions increasingly use variable damage and powers to make enemies less predictable.

----

The Undead Gunslinger challenges you to a duel. Do you accept, or do you send the henchman?
